Off Calendar Aug-14-2020 Continued To Nov-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2020 Continued To Sep-28-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion Against Defendant Que Moles, Ltd.
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Glenn W. Calsada, esq. (818-477-0314) for Defendant via courtcall.

No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FLEXIBLE FUNDING LTD. LIABILITY CO.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion Against Defendant Que Moles, Ltd.

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding what services the assignor (Galca/TSPS) provided to defendant and what sum is owed. Plaintiff as assignee alleges that assignor placed temporary employees with defendant and seeks to collect $163,485.17 plus pre-judgment interest for services rendered. (Complaint, par. 9.) Mr. Deluca of plaintiff assignee declares that assignor provided "personnel services" to defendant. (Deluca Dec., par. 16.) Defendant presents evidence showing that TS Personnel Services, Inc. simply took over payroll services and TSPS never placed temporary workers with defendant. (Huerta Dec., pars. 7 and 13.) Defendant further shows that "[t]here were NEVER any unpaid invoices." (Huerta Dec., par. 7.) Given this dispute, the court cannot grant summary adjudication in favor of plaintiff.

In addition, it appears that plaintiff lacks capacity to maintain this action. The evidence establishes that assignor Galca was a suspended corporation as of February 1, 2017. (Calsada Dec., par. 10, Ex. 12 [Bates Stamp 160-162].) There is no evidence that assignor's taxes were ever paid and there is no certificate of revivor from the Franchise Tax Board in the record. Cal-Western Business Services, Inc. v. Corning Capital Group (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 304, which plaintiff does not address although defendant raised it in its opposition papers, is controlling. It holds that an assignee stands in the shoes of the assignor and cannot prosecute a claim on behalf of a suspended assignor corporation. (Id. at 311 [affirming order striking assignee's complaint and dismissing action].) Plaintiff's reliance in its untimely reply brief on an unpublished decision (Reply at 3-4) is improper. (See Cal. R. Ct. 8.1115(a).)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
